name: Guangzhou
intro: Guangzhou suits travelers who want Cantonese food culture, a major southern transport hub, and a city that feels practical rather than checklist-heavy.
seoTitle: Guangzhou Travel Guide for First-Time Visitors
seoDescription: Plan a first trip to Guangzhou with practical advice on food-focused neighborhoods, trip pace, and how to use the city within a larger South China route.
tripPace: Best as a practical food and city-life stop with room for evenings.
highlights: Strong for travelers who care more about food, neighborhoods, and regional culture than about headline landmark collecting.
Works especially well as a transport-smart stop in a South China itinerary.
Gives the site a different city archetype from Beijing or Shanghai because it is less about iconic first-postcard sights.

planningPriorities.title: Decide whether Guangzhou is a main stop or a connector city
planningPriorities.description: The city can work either as a standalone food-focused destination or as a practical anchor in a broader southern route.

planningPriorities.title: Plan around neighborhoods and meals, not just attractions
planningPriorities.description: Guangzhou becomes much more rewarding when readers think in terms of districts, evening food plans, and transit convenience.

planningPriorities.title: Shape nearby pairings early
planningPriorities.description: If Hong Kong, Shenzhen, or another Pearl River Delta city is part of the trip, transport logic should be decided before the day-by-day plan.

transportNote: Guangzhou is especially useful in itineraries where rail connections and regional combinations matter as much as the city itself.
